zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln於1080年出生在英國林可爾史爾時，他的父親Sir Gilbert De Gaunt33歲，母親Alice De Montfort30歲。他於1110年之前與Maud de Penthièvre在英國林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1139年8月22日在英國諾圖波藍德享年59歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾巴德尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln于1080年出生在英国林可爾史爾时，他的父亲Sir Gilbert De Gaunt33岁，母亲Alice De Montfort30岁。他于1110年之前与Maud de Penthièvre在英国林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1139年8月22日在英国諾圖波藍德享年59岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾巴德尼。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gaunt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Gant  .

variants.name: Gant
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ghent in Flanders, Belgium.
English: from Middle English gaunt, gant ‘slim, slender; drawn, thin and angular’.
North German: probably a variant of   Gans   ‘goose’.

variants.name: Gantt
meanings: English: altered form of   Gaunt  .

variants.name: Gauntt
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Gaunt  .

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Lincoln
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Lincoln, so named from an original British name Lindo- ‘lake’ + Latin colonia ‘settlement, colony’. The place was an important administrative center during the Roman occupation of Britain and in the Middle Ages it was a center for the manufacture of cloth, including the famous ‘Lincoln green’.
African American: adoption of the surname in 1 above, in honor of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the US, who abolished slavery.
History: Abraham Lincoln (1809–65), 16th president of the US, was the son of an illiterate laborer, descended from Samuel Lincoln, who had emigrated from England to MA in 1637.

variants.name: Linkins
meanings: English: variant of Linkin, which itself is a form of Lincoln , prevalent in Kent.

variants.name: Lindsay
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Lindsey in Lincolnshire, England. This is first found in the form Lindissi, apparently a derivative of the British name of Lincoln . To this was later added the Old English element ēg ‘island’, since the place was virtually cut off by the surrounding fenland. The surname was taken to Scotland at an early date and is the name of an important and powerful Scottish family.
Irish: adopted as an equivalent ofvarious Gaelic names (see   Lindsey  ).

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln was born in 1080, in Lincolnshire, England, his father, Sir Gilbert De Gaunt, was 33 and his mother, Alice De Montfort, was 30. He married Maud de Penthièvre before 1110, in Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 22 August 1139, in Northumberland, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Bardney, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln est né en 1080, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Gilbert De Gaunt, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Alice De Montfort, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Penthièvre avant 1110, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 22 août 1139, à Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Bardney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln 1080 in Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Gilbert De Gaunt 33 und seine Mutter Alice De Montfort 30. Er hat vor 1110 in Lincolnshire, England Maud de Penthièvre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 22. August 1139 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Northumberland, England und wurde in Bardney, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln è nato il nel 1080 nel Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Gilbert De Gaunt, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Alice De Montfort, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Maud de Penthièvre prima del 1110, nel Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 22 agosto 1139, nel Northumberland, England, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Bardney, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincolnが1080年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Gilbert De Gauntは33歳，母Alice De Montfortは30歳であった。1110年以前にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでMaud de Penthièvreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1139年8月22日にイギリスノースウンバーランドで59歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアバードニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln(이)가 1080년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Gilbert De Gaunt(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice De Montfort(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1110년 이전 영국 링코실르에서 Maud de Penthièvre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1139년 8월 22일 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 바드니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln nasceu em 1080, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Gilbert De Gaunt, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Alice De Montfort, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud de Penthièvre antes 1110, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de agosto de 1139, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Bardney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln родился в 1080 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Gilbert De Gaunt было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Alice De Montfort, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Penthièvre перед 1110 г. в местности линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 22 августа 1139 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности бардни, линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln nació en 1080, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Gilbert De Gaunt, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Alice De Montfort, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Maud de Penthièvre antes de 1110, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 22 de agosto de 1139, en Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Bardney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
